req.title: Audit log access (negative)
req.statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that a privacy-officer can retrieve AuditEvent records scoped to their own organisation and is forbidden (403) from accessing another organisation's records. Five test cases are designed: (1) EP-positive: officer queries own org and receives 200 with matching records; (2) NEG: officer queries a different org's records and receives 403; (3) BVA: officer queries with an organisation filter that sits exactly on the boundary between own-org and foreign-org identifiers (single-character difference); (4) DT: missing mandatory Authorization header yields 401; (5) STC: token revoked mid-session triggers 401/403 on subsequent request, covering a conflicting state transition as called out in the requirement error pathways.

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_can_access_own_org_audit_logs
preconditions: Authenticated privacy-officer token exists for org-A
At least one AuditEvent record exists scoped to org-A
actions: Obtain valid Bearer token for privacy-officer in org-A via <get_token_fixture>
expected: Response body is a FHIR Bundle
All entries have agent organisation reference equal to org-A
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_privacy_officer_can_access_own_org_audit_logs(
    api_client,          # <fixture> configured requests.Session with base_url
    privacy_officer_token_org_a,  # <fixture> returns Bearer token string
    ensure_audit_event_org_a,     # <fixture> seeds >=1 AuditEvent for org-A
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {privacy_officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = api_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"organization": "org-A"}, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    assert body.get("total", 0) >= 1

    for entry in body.get("entry", []):
        resource = entry.get("resource", {})
        agents = resource.get("agent", [])
        org_refs = [
            a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "")
            for a in agents
        ]
        assert any("org-A" in ref for ref in org_refs), (
            f"Entry contains agent not in org-A: {org_refs}"
        )

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_denied_access_to_foreign_org_audit_logs
preconditions: Authenticated privacy-officer token exists for org-A
org-B exists and has AuditEvent records
Officer has no role in org-B
actions: Obtain valid Bearer token for privacy-officer in org-A via <get_token_fixture>
Inspect HTTP status code and response body
expected: No AuditEvent records from org-B are disclosed
trace: R-HLT-064: cross-organisation access shall be forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_privacy_officer_denied_access_to_foreign_org_audit_logs(
    api_client,
    privacy_officer_token_org_a,
    ensure_audit_event_org_b,  # <fixture> seeds >=1 AuditEvent for org-B
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {privacy_officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = api_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"organization": "org-B"}, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", (
        "Expected OperationOutcome on forbidden access"
    )
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert len(issues) >= 1
    severities = [i.get("severity") for i in issues]
    assert "error" in severities, f"No error-level issue found: {severities}"

tests.name: test_audit_log_access_boundary_between_own_and_foreign_org_id
preconditions: Authenticated privacy-officer token exists for org-A
Organisation identifier 'org-A1' exists and belongs to a different tenant
Officer has no role in org-A1
actions: Obtain valid Bearer token for privacy-officer in org-A via <get_token_fixture>
GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-A1 with Authorization header (org-A1 differs from org-A by one character)
expected: No AuditEvent records from org-A1 disclosed
System does not conflate org-A with org-A1 due to prefix matching
trace: R-HLT-064: cross-organisation access shall be forbidden
BVA applied to org identifier boundary (adjacent string)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_audit_log_access_boundary_between_own_and_foreign_org_id(
    api_client,
    privacy_officer_token_org_a,
    ensure_audit_event_org_a1,  # <fixture> seeds AuditEvent for org-A1
):
    # org-A1 is adjacent to org-A by exactly one appended character.
    # A naive prefix-match bug would grant access; this test catches it.
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {privacy_officer_token_org_a}"}
    resp = api_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"organization": "org-A1"}, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Boundary org-A1 must be forbidden for org-A officer; got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    # Ensure no records leaked even in error payload
    assert "entry" not in body, "AuditEvent entries must not appear in 403 response"

tests.name: test_audit_log_request_without_authorization_header_rejected
preconditions: No Authorization header is provided (missing mandatory element)
actions: Inspect HTTP status code and response body
expected: Response body is FHIR OperationOutcome with severity 'error'
trace: R-HLT-064: missing mandatory elements negative path
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_audit_log_request_without_authorization_header_rejected(
    api_client,
):
    # Decision table: row where Authorization = absent => 401
    resp = api_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"organization": "org-A"})

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert "WWW-Authenticate" in resp.headers, (
        "WWW-Authenticate header must be present on 401"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues), (
        f"Expected error-severity issue, got: {issues}"
    )

tests.name: test_audit_log_access_denied_after_token_revocation
preconditions: Authenticated privacy-officer token for org-A has been issued
Token is valid and confirmed working (state: ACTIVE)
actions: Obtain valid Bearer token for privacy-officer in org-A
Confirm token works: GET /AuditEvent?organization=org-A returns 200 (state: ACTIVE)
Revoke the token via <revoke_token_helper> (state transition: ACTIVE -> REVOKED)
expected: After revocation, second request returns HTTP 401 or 403 (state: REVOKED)
No AuditEvent records disclosed on second request
trace: R-HLT-064: conflicting state transitions negative path
STC: ACTIVE -> REVOKED state transition coverage
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_audit_log_access_denied_after_token_revocation(
    api_client,
    privacy_officer_token_org_a,
    revoke_token,            # <helper_fn>(token) -> None, calls revocation endpoint
    ensure_audit_event_org_a,
):
    token = privacy_officer_token_org_a
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    # State: ACTIVE - baseline confirmation
    resp_before = api_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"organization": "org-A"}, headers=headers)
    assert resp_before.status_code == 200, (
        f"Pre-revocation request must succeed; got {resp_before.status_code}"
    )

    # Transition ACTIVE -> REVOKED
    revoke_token(token)

    # State: REVOKED - access must be denied
    resp_after = api_client.get("/AuditEvent", params={"organization": "org-A"}, headers=headers)
    assert resp_after.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Post-revocation must be 401/403; got {resp_after.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp_after.json()
    assert "entry" not in body, "Revoked token must not yield AuditEvent entries"
